Jamaica Market Penetration — Propensity Model Summary.

The propensity-to-buy model predicts the probability that a household in a given
enumeration district (ED) will become a customer. It was trained using XGBoost
(gradient boosted trees) with 5-fold stratified cross-validation.

Overall market penetration for Jamaica: 0.2%
(ratio of current customers to addressable market, aggregated across all 116 EDs).

Feature importance ranking (what drives conversion likelihood):
(1) competitor outlet density — importance 0.424; (2) log population density — importance 0.154; (3) payday sensitivity index — importance 0.082; (4) normalised proximity to nearest outlet — importance 0.081; (5) social influence proxy (income level + population density) — importance 0.072; (6) road accessibility score — importance 0.070.

The two strongest drivers are brand awareness and social influence — both are latent
variables estimated via observable proxies. This means the most effective market
penetration levers are: (1) increasing outlet presence to build brand visibility,
and (2) targeting high-density, higher-income communities first to trigger peer
adoption effects.

Competitor density is the primary conversion suppressor. EDs with high competitor
density require either a pricing or differentiation strategy before promotional spend.

Model performance: CV AUC on training folds reflects discrimination ability between
converters and non-converters. SHAP values were computed on a representative subsample
of 2,000 households.
